anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Dhammatalks' note to SN 22.59 points to texts about a kind of consciousness outside the aggregate range of all, and Thanissaro argues not-self is strategic rather than a denial of all remainder.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Reduces confidence and requires the residue field to distinguish aggregate remainder from non-aggregate liberation language.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If early Buddhist materials allow an unestablished or unobjectified dimension beyond aggregate consciousness, then SN 22.59 cannot be used as simple no-remainder policy. It only blocks aggregate appropriation.

claim_units: Both traditions unsettle identification with ordinary experience.
The key comparison unit is residue policy after negation.
Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23 permits an unobjectifiable seer-knower as remainder.
SN 22.59 applies not-self analysis to consciousness and does not authorize an aggregate remainder.
The strain is not just self versus no-self, but whether de-objectification licenses residue.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Therapies that strengthen observer-self language should differ from therapies that deconstruct observer-self language in how clients report agency and identity after decentering.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: After negative inquiry dissolves ordinary roles and narratives, a method either authorizes a stable observing self, recursively investigates it, or brackets it pragmatically.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Observer-self and no-self style protocols yield identical reports and clinical outcomes with no distinct residue policy effects.

exact_claim: The precise Advaita-Buddhist comparison should ask what each path permits to remain after negation: Brihadaranyaka preserves an unobjectifiable seer-knower, while SN 22.59 withholds authorization from any aggregate including consciousness.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Advaita instructions should more often say rest as, recognize, or abide as awareness, while vipassana instructions should more often say observe knowing, consciousness, or awareness as arising, passing, empty, or not-self.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practice manuals should diverge at the point where attention tries to identify awareness itself.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Coding Ramana, Shankara-lineage, Mahasi, Thai Forest, and early sutta practice instructions.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Manuals across both traditions use the same instruction at that point, or both authorize and de-authorize awareness in similar ways.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: After reporting contentless or objectless awareness, Advaita-trained practitioners should more often use Self or awareness-as-ground language, while Buddhist-trained practitioners should resist ontological upgrade.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Practitioners trained in different residue policies should differ in post-negation interpretation even when phenomenological reports are similar.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews after self-inquiry, vipassana, and open-awareness retreats.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Training tradition does not predict interpretation, or reports do not pass through any recognizable residue question.

literature_search_queries: Advaita Buddhism witness consciousness not-self comparison Atman anatman
Shankara witness consciousness deep sleep Buddhist no self comparison
Thanissaro not-self strategy no self aggregates consciousness
Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23 unseen seer no other knower

near_neighbors.difference: The Lumenary idea compares this with early Buddhist aggregate analysis and names the comparison residue policy.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the preserved witness component.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for the Advaita side. It presents witnessing consciousness as distinct from mind and cognition, self-illuminating, and continuous through waking, dreaming, and deep sleep.

near_neighbors.difference: Residue policy is a more operational audit field than Loy's broader comparative essay.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Loy directly compares nirvana and moksha, objectless consciousness, Atman-Brahman, and Buddhist cessation, including the difficulty of calling nondual awareness consciousness.
near_neighbors.source: David Loy, Enlightenment in Buddhism and Advaita Vedanta, https://buddhism.lib.ntu.edu.tw/FULLTEXT/JR-AN/26715.htm

near_neighbors.difference: The Lumenary idea focuses on whether a remainder is authorized in a specific comparison, not on the full Pali Canon hermeneutic.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close anomaly and prior art. Thanissaro frames not-self as a liberation strategy rather than a metaphysical claim that there is no self.

near_neighbors.difference: It does not turn the comparison with Buddhism into a residue-authorization rubric.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. It explains Advaita's deconstruction of self-objectification and the claim that consciousness is self-disclosing and never an object.
near_neighbors.source: Matthias Rose, Prakasa: Advaitic consciousness as presence, https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11097-020-09690-2

next_loop_instructions: Test the rubric on Brihadaranyaka 2.4, 2.5, 3.7.23, SN 22.59, and SN 22.95 before broader claims.
Separate antaryamin cosmology from later Advaita witness practice.
Add values for aggregate remainder, non-aggregate remainder, strategic silence, and undecidable.
Do not publish without Pali and Sanskrit term checks.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.76 to about 0.57. The contrast is familiar, but the residue-authorization field is a useful operational refinement.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: They say this is the standard Atman/anatta distinction or a familiar Advaita versus Buddhist contrast with a new label.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: They say the residue field clarifies specific teacher instructions and prevents false nondual equivalence.
questions: In your practice, what is the student told to do when only awareness or knowing seems to remain?
Is awareness recognized as Self, investigated as another phenomenon, or left strategically unclassified?
Does residue policy name a real practice difference or only redescribe familiar doctrine?

primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: Moderate to high. The idea risks importing later Advaita sakshin doctrine into an older Upanishadic passage and treating one early Buddhist discourse as all Buddhist self-analysis.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Close prior art includes Advaita-Buddhism comparison, Shankara on witnessing consciousness, Buddhist not-self strategy debates, and scholarship on consciousness without content. The specific phrase residue policy was not found, but the structural contrast is well known.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: The contrast is textually real but needs narrowing. Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23 is not merely a meditation report after negation. It is part of the antaryamin sequence, where the inner ruler is within cosmic elements, beings, organs, mind, and knowledge. SN 22.59 is not a general ontology of every possible remainder; it is an analysis of the aggregates as unfit for appropriation. The proposed residue policy works if it is limited to the comparison between authorized Self-language and non-appropriation of aggregate consciousness.

title: Residue Policy in Negative Self-Practice
unlike_statement: Unlike standard self versus no-self comparisons, this idea proposes a smaller operational field: after ordinary identifications are negated, record whether the tradition authorizes, tests, refuses, or leaves undecided any remainder.
